summary: Web Push notification implementation fully tested. All 12 backend API tests passed. Frontend Settings page with Notifications section verified with all UI elements present.
passed_tests: GET /api/push/vapid-public-key - Returns valid VAPID public key (87 chars)
POST /api/push/subscribe - Accepts valid browser subscription with endpoint and keys
PUT /api/push/settings - Updates notification preferences (glances, drinks, messages, matches)
GET /api/push/pending - Returns list of pending notifications
Glance action queues push notification for target user
Service worker file sw.js accessible at frontend root (HTTP 200)
Settings page shows Notifications section with Bell icon
Settings page shows 'Enable Push Notifications' toggle with data-testid='push-enabled-toggle'
Settings page shows 'Get notified when the app is closed' description
All 4 push sub-toggles visible: Glances, Drink Offers, Messages, Matches
Sub-toggles have proper data-testid attributes (push-glances-toggle, push-drinks-toggle, push-messages-toggle, push-matches-toggle)
Service Worker registration confirmed in browser console (SW registered)

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All Settings page Notifications elements verified

test_credentials.note: Register new users dynamically or use test_push_1773441411@test.com / TestPass123!

seed_data_creation: None needed - tests use dynamic user registration
context_for_next_testing_agent: Web Push implementation uses pywebpush library with VAPID keys configured in backend/.env. Service worker at /app/frontend/public/sw.js handles push events and notification clicks. Push subscriptions stored in 'push_subscriptions' collection, settings in 'push_settings', and queued notifications in 'push_queue'. Frontend Settings page at /settings has Notifications section with master toggle and 4 sub-toggles for glances/drinks/messages/matches. All toggles have proper data-testid attributes for testing.

features_verified.service_worker: sw.js exists at /app/frontend/public/sw.js and accessible via HTTPS
features_verified.push_subscribe: POST /api/push/subscribe stores subscription with endpoint and keys
features_verified.push_delivery: send_push_notification() uses pywebpush to deliver to subscribed users
features_verified.settings_ui: Settings page shows Notifications section with enable toggle and 4 sub-toggles
